Which of the following is NOT a valid difference between @Inject and @Autowired annotations?
options: @Inject annotation is part of Java EE 7’s Context and Dependency Injection framework while @Autowired is the Spring Framework’s own implementation
@Autowired is a legacy annotation whereas @Inject is its latest version.
There is no difference and both can be used interchangeably.
correct: @Autowired is a legacy annotation whereas @Inject is its latest version.

question:

execution(* *..AccountServiceImpl.update(..)) && execution(* *..ClientServiceImpl.update(..))

Matches any update methods of the 2 classes, whatever the arguments and method visibility

question: Select the right statement about referring a Spring configuration file inside the package com.example.myapp in the following example: ApplicationContext context = new ClassPathXmlApplicationContext("classpath:/com.example.myapp.config.xml");
options: The classpath: prefix could be omitted
Package name using the dot character is not well formatted
The slash character preceding com.example could be omitted
All methods, regardless of their visibility
Two beans are defined: a data source and a repository
RequestMethod.GET method is more accurate than POST
@ResponseBody could be removed (it is redundant with @RestController)
Returning the 201 HTTP status code is better
correct: RequestMethod.GET method is more accurate than POST

question: What is an advice in Spring AOP?
options: An action taken by an aspect at a particular join point
A point during the execution of a program
A predicate that matches join points
correct: An action taken by an aspect at a particular join point

question: Which of the following method signatures is easiest to write a unit test for in Spring MVC?
options: @RequestMapping("/displayAccount") String displayAccount(@RequestParam("accountId") int id, Model model)
void displayAccount(HttpServletRequest req, HttpServletResponse resp) throws ServletException, IOException
void displayAccount(HttpServletRequest req, HttpSession session) throws ServletException, IOException
@RequestMapping("/displayAccount") String displayAccount(@PathVariable("accountId") int id, Model model)
correct: @RequestMapping("/displayAccount") String displayAccount(@RequestParam("accountId") int id, Model model)

options: Spring MVC framework is Model-Value-Class architecture and used to bind model data with values.

question: How do you reload your changes on Spring Boot without having to restart the server?
